content: We can reframe ASMR (Agentic search and memory retrieval) as an intrinsic curiosity-driven architecture, where the ICM curiosity mechanism rewards the agent for actively searching under-explored segments of its long-term memory, rather than only rewarding it for exploring new external environments. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where the agent continuously improves the organization and accessibility of its own long-term memory without external task-specific rewards.
